text: To set effective project goals, start by using the SMART framework. This means goals should be specific, measurable, achievable, relevant, and time-bound. Next, break these goals into smaller milestones, which are key stages that show progress. Each milestone should lead to clear deliverables, such as a completed report or a prototype. Finally, ensure stakeholder alignment by discussing expectations with everyone involved. This avoids misunderstandings and keeps the project on track. For example, a SMART goal might be “Increase website traffic by 20% within three months,” with monthly milestones for content launches and a final deliverable of the analytics report.
